item.title: Working on a markdown -> html parser
item.description:
Hello World!

Working on a markdown to html parser but only with regexes, because why not! Thought this would be a good way to learn more about regexes.

Have a nice day!!

https://samiuljoy.github.io

item.title: I f**d up my phone
item.description:
Hello World!

Well, I tried to do a system reset and dump stuffs to the system partition. The network firmware or radio doesn't seem to be working. I was thinking about doing a system reset for past couple of days, good thing is I was able to back everything up before all of this. I think improper file permission lead to this stage. Hmm..let's see if I can fix this.

Have a nice day!!

https://samiuljoy.github.io

item.title: Figured out a way to disable transitions and animations
item.description:
Hello World!

Was able to disable transitions and animations customizing some value under /res/values/ to @null or 0. Fenix now seems to be faster. Way faster than before.

Also, started watching Serial Experiments Lain and Ergo Proxy. Quite fascinating.

Have a nice day!!

https://samiuljoy.github.io

item.title: Figuring out a way to disable transition effects on fenix Android
item.description:
Hello World!!

Other Android browsers like brave and chrome have little to no animation in their UI. Which in my opnion is a good thing. They actually took into consideration about those android devices that are running old hardwares. Fenix on the other hand, has a bit of transition and animation going on here and there in thier UI. Which looks good, definitely and adds a bit of premium feel to it, however imo, this is useless. Hence fiddling around with the source codes to find a way to turn it off. On my newer phone, turning off animations from the developer options seems to negate transition effects, however on the older device, this doesn't turn off transitions. Let's see if I can find a way to manipulate transitions and animations.

Have a nice day!! and love and support open-source. If Fenix wasn't open-sourced, couldn't even edit the source codes to begin with!

samiuljoy.github.io

item.title: Performance improvement for firefox in Android
item.description:
Hello world!!

Recompiling firefox after removing couple of stuffs seems to improve performance. Although this is hardly noticeable on modern devices above 2GB of ram, however, on older device, this change is apparent. Also, the latest version of firefox pre-release(85.0-beta-7) performance improvement is also seen(by default without manual intervention). Looking forward to a better performance from firefox-fenix.

Have a nice day!!

samiuljoy.github.io

item.title: Fixed the mozilla.location issue while recompiling + more
item.description:
Hello world!!

Subject: Fixed the mozilla.location service issue mentioned in the last post and reliving nostalgia!!

I was able to fix the location.service url issue. Turns out, replacing 0.0.0.0 with http://0.0.0.0 does the job perfectly.

Also started playing Beneath a Steel Sky on my Android device. I remember playing it back in 2014 on my Ubuntu machine. Good to know that https://scummvm.org made it possible to port it on Android!! That's pretty cool!!

Have a nice day!

samiuljoy.github.io.

item.title: Just got familiar with gopher and gemini
item.description:
Hello world!!

Subject: Got myself familiar with Gopher and gemini

Gopher and Gemini are two internet protocols that existed back in 1965. People used these prior to http/s web standard. It eventually went into abyss. Nevertheless, just got introduced to it through fediverse, and thought it'd be a good idea to check it out. I seem to enjoy it so far. Also got to know couple of people from there and followed them on fediverse. I've came across pretty decent people on gemini and gopher. Worth checking out.

It's good to know that something alternative and more simplistic exists out there aside from the modern web, aside from the crowd, and it never hurts to check these alternative platforms out. You never know, might end up liking it ^..^

Have a wonderful day!!

samiuljoy.github.io.

item.title: My first rss post!!
item.description:
Hello world!! This is my first rss feed post!!!

Subject: Today I customized an app for the first time.

Hello world!! For the first time in my life I was able to extract the source codes from an app, edit some color properties, customized it to my liking, and repacked the app and signed it and installed it and voila!! There was my app!! It feels good ^_^

The name of the app was overchan, just incase anyone wondering ^_^

Have a wonderful day!!

Visit samiuljoy.github.io.
